fixes.description: Added comparison_baseline='epistasis' with network_baseline_modifiers={Photoperiod_LD: 0.0} to all 8 SD-condition tests (T002, T010, T015, T055, T056, T073, T076, T077). The validator now compares each mutant-under-SD perturbation against a WT-under-SD baseline rather than against the default WT-under-LD-permissive baseline.
fixes.reason: SD tests use gene_modifier={Photoperiod_LD: 0.0} to represent short-day conditions. In iter 2 these tests compared to the default WT baseline (all sources=1.0, LD-like). That mixed two changes (photoperiod off AND gene KO) into one comparison, producing strong 'decreased' predictions where biology said 'unchanged' (T010 SbPhyC SD, T076 SbGhd7 SD, T077 Ma2_SMYD SD) or 'increased' (T015 SbGI SD). Using epistasis baseline with Photoperiod_LD=0 isolates the gene-KO effect: both baseline and perturbed cascades have LD=0, so the cascade collapse is common-mode and subtracts away. The gene KO's residual effect then matches biology.
fixes.biological_justification: Biological comparison convention: sbphyC under SD is compared to WT under SD (not to WT under LD). The paper-reported effects ('unchanged' for photoperiod-insensitive backgrounds in SD, 'increased' for sbgi SD) are relative to SD controls in the same experiment. The algebraic framework's default WT baseline assumes permissive (LD-equivalent) conditions, which mixes photoperiod with gene effects. Epistasis baseline encodes the paired SD-vs-SD comparison used in the underlying papers.
fixes.predicted_impact: +2-4 tests across methods; expected to fix T010/T015/T076/T077.

results.reason: Best-method (RWR) improved from 85.7% to 87.0% (+1.3%, above 0.5% threshold). Algebraic +2.6% (gained T015, T076), ODE +5.2% (gained T010, T015, T056, T076), RWR +1.3% (gained T015). Zero regressions on any method.
